examples.title: Datagram Packet Forwarding Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm demonstrating how each packet is independently routed in datagram switching
examples.code:
PROCEDURE DatagramForward(packet)
BEGIN
 WHILE packet not at destination DO
 // Extract destination address from packet header
 destAddr ← packet.header.destinationAddress
 
 // Routing table lookup for next hop
 nextHop ← RoutingTable.lookup(destAddr)
 
 // Store-and-forward: receive completely before sending
 routerBuffer.receive(packet)
 
 // Check for errors before forwarding
 IF packet.checksumValid THEN
 routerBuffer.send(nextHop, packet)
 ELSE
 discard(packet)
 requestRetransmission(packet.seqNumber)
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Deliver to upper layer at destination
 deliverToApplication(packet.payload)
END
examples.explanation: Each packet is routed independently using destination address. No connection state is maintained between packets. This pseudocode shows the store-and-forward mechanism and error checking at each router.

examples.title: Virtual Circuit Establishment and Data Transfer
examples.description: Algorithm showing setup phase and packet transfer in virtual circuit switching
examples.code:
// Phase 1: Virtual Circuit Setup
PROCEDURE EstablishVirtualCircuit(source, dest)
BEGIN
 // Send connection request packet
 setupPacket ← createPacket(source, dest, SETUP_REQUEST)
 path ← RouteFromSourceToDest(source, dest)
 
 // Reserve resources along the path
 FOR each router in path DO
 router.allocateBuffer(vcID)
 router.allocateBandwidth(vcID)
 END FOR
 
 // Return virtual circuit identifier
 RETURN vcID
END

// Phase 2: Data Transfer using VCI
PROCEDURE VirtualCircuitTransfer(data, vcID)
BEGIN
 // Divide data into packets with VCI
 packets ← FragmentData(data)
 
 FOR each packet in packets DO
 packet.header.vcIdentifier ← vcID
 packet.header.sequenceNum ← currentSeq
 
 // Forward using VCI (no routing lookup needed)
 nextHop ← precomputedPath[currentHop]
 routerBuffer.send(nextHop, packet)
 currentHop ← currentHop + 1
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Virtual circuit establishes a path first with resource allocation. All subsequent packets use the Virtual Circuit Identifier (VCI) for fast forwarding without routing table lookups.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) setup, O(1) per packet forward

examples.title: Packet Switching Delay Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate total delay in store-and-forward packet switching
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CalculatePacketSwitchingDelay(messageSize, packetSize, 
 propagationDelay, transmissionDelay, numHops)
BEGIN
 // Calculate number of packets
 numPackets ← ceil(messageSize / packetSize)
 
 // Transmission time for one packet
 transTimePerPacket ← packetSize / transmissionRate
 
 // Total delay using store-and-forward at N hops
 // First packet takes: (N × propagation) + (N × transTimePerPacket)
 // Remaining packets: (numPackets - 1) × transTimePerPacket
 
 firstPacketDelay ← numHops * (propagationDelay + transTimePerPacket)
 subsequentPacketsDelay ← (numPackets - 1) * transTimePerPacket
 
 totalDelay ← firstPacketDelay + subsequentPacketsDelay
 
 RETURN totalDelay
END

// Example calculation
messageSize ← 8000 bits
packetSize ← 1000 bits
numHops ← 3
transRate ← 1000000 bits/sec // 1 Mbps
propDelay ← 0.02 sec // 20 ms

transTime ← 1000 / 1000000 = 0.001 sec // 1 ms per packet
numPackets ← 8
totalDelay ← 3*(0.02+0.001) + 7*(0.001) = 0.076 sec = 76 ms
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how to calculate cumulative delays in packet-switched networks, accounting for pipeline effect where subsequent packets are transmitted while the first packet is still being forwarded.
